Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(5 comments)

I'm a bit concerned about this change, since there are comments to the effect that the increment of the LAST_ID counter needs to be done during the transaction, and that is no longer the case.

Also, opo_reserved appears to no longer be useful, and at a minimum that should be removed in this patch instead of being left behind and only appearing to be used.

Alex, could you please take a look w.r.t. correctness from the LAST_ID/transaction/recovery POV.

Patch Set 1:

"I'm a bit concerned about this change, since there are comments to the effect that the increment of the LAST_ID counter needs to be done during the transaction, and that is no longer the case."

I am not sure I follow that comments, unfortunately it only said allocate FID inside an open transaction, but no reason. I might miss sth. Alex, could you please clarify here?

Another way to fix this problem is to remember the ost idx in LOD (might need a few extra allocation, a bit reluctant to do that if there no other choice)

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

the order we do declarations don't match execution order. I don't think we really want to move FID assignment to the declaration as that would lead to orphans (gaps).
don't we know sequence at declaration?

Patch Set 1:

"don't we know sequence at declaration?" we only know sequence in OSP layer, but that is not where we cache the FLD entries. 

Then another way to fix this is to remember the ost_index inside the lod_object, then it means a few extra bytes for lod_object.

Patch Set 1:

hmm, why do we need to store anything? probably I'm missing some important detail here..

Patch Set 1:

Alex, the root driver for this patch is because we are doing RPCs to the FLD server in the middle of an open transaction, which can cause deadlocks. Di created a patch at my request (http://review.whamcloud.com/23111 "LU-8250 osd: add journal info check") to catch cases where an RPC is sent with an open transaction, and it caught a few places doing this.

My thought is that we could do the FLD lookups during declare in advance of starting the transaction, and that should avoid any deadlock?  Even if we don't know the exact FID, we should at least know the right SEQ numbers. In extremely rare cases when the SEQ wraps and the next SEQ is on a different server, we might send an RPC with the transaction open, but as long as this doesn't trigger an LASSERT() we should be fine.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Alex, is this why you were writing that we don't need to store anything, because this info is already available?  I agree with Di that we don't really want to allocate and store the OST index for every object being allocated, since the code already knows it.  It is just a matter of making this value available in the right manner.

Alex, can you please propose how the device index should be exposed to the upper layers.  I don't like getting complexity in the code like this to work around the layering, when we already have the index available.

Patch Set 2: -Code-Review

the root of the issue is a missing entry in the local FLD cache, right? we can populate it at declaration calling lod_fld_lookup() with any FID in that sequence?

Patch Set 2:

TBH, I thought about the same thing, but there is no fld client in OSP, where we can only see FID in declare phase. I will see what I can do.

Patch Set 2:

hmm, maybe we can, let me cook a patch.

parents.subject: LU-8246 ldlm: Do not grant a lock twice in a race

commit.subject: LU-8704 lod: Not sending RPC for lookup ost idx
commit.message:
LU-8704 lod: Not sending RPC for lookup ost idx

To avoid sending FLD lookup RPC inside the transaction,
let's find out ost index by lod/osp internal structure.

Signed-off-by: Di Wang <di.wang@intel.com>
Change-Id: If85cd769ed6eed540fffff130da484e002a09187
